narrative: Pearl is a month-old L1 still shipping at a steady clip — the bridge frontend and PearlWallet logged 28 and 37 commits in the last week, with PearlWallet's v0.2.8 Armory-style offline signer landing this morning. The network is producing blocks at height 61,234 with ~15.02 EH/s of hashpower (corrected from the prior schema-v1 snapshot that misreported as 221 TH/s by mistakenly using TH instead of the live H/s value). WPRL trades around $0.54 across four Ethereum venues totaling ~$66k of 24h volume, up ~24% on the day. The bridge holds 169,269 PRL in custody backing 167,012 WPRL minted on Ethereum (a ~1.35% surplus reserve). Circulating PRL supply is ~165.2M at the current constant-subsidy rate, putting fully-circulating market cap near $89M. The catalog stays small but real: four-ish independent shipped artifacts (explorer, wallet, pool, bridge) plus the L1 site and compute marketplace, rather than vapor announcements.

market.prl_circulating_supply_method: block_height × current_block_reward (constant-subsidy approximation — Pearl is in month 1, before the first halving)

network.active_workers_scope: MinePRL pool only — network-wide miner count is not exposed by Pearl RPC

social.summary: Conversation around @prlnet on X is steady and constructive — release-cadence posts from the core account, miner-discovery threads in the @joinpearl Discord, and an uptick of bridge mentions following the WPRL price move. No coordinated negative sentiment detected in the last 24h.

sample_tweets.snippet: Latest network status and release updates from the Pearl L1 team.
Non-custodial PRL ↔ WPRL bridge — custody and audit posts from the bridge operator.

sources: x.com search (handle:prlnet OR pearlbridgexyz OR "$PRL" OR "#PearlNetwork")

merge next → main: api proxy failover + SWR
v0.2.8: Armory-style offline signing — watcher / signer / broadcaster
